project.name: TeleMed
project.description: Telemedicine platform for online doctor consultations, prescription management, and medical record storage.
project.notes: HIPAA-regulated. Patient data (MedicalRecord, Prescription, Diagnosis) is PHI. All PHI must be encrypted at rest. BAA required with all vendors.

pages.name: Login
pages.description: Public login page with email/password and optional TOTP code field.

pages.name: PatientDashboard
pages.description: Patient home screen showing upcoming appointments and recently accessed medical records.

pages.name: DoctorDashboard
pages.description: Doctor home screen showing today's scheduled appointments and prescriptions pending review.

pages.name: AppointmentBook
pages.description: Allows a patient to search for an available doctor and book a video consultation time slot.

pages.name: VideoConsult
pages.description: Live video consultation room powered by Daily.co embed. Doctor can take session notes in the side panel.

pages.name: MedicalRecords
pages.description: Patient view of all medical records. Records are read-only for the patient; upload triggers a doctor review workflow.

pages.name: Prescriptions
pages.description: Shared prescriptions view. Patients see their own prescriptions (read-only). Doctors can add new prescriptions for their patients.

pages.name: AdminPanel
pages.description: Admin-only panel for managing user accounts, viewing system audit logs, and performing compliance reviews.

fields.name: totp_code
fields.notes: Optional for patients; required at middleware level for doctor and admin roles

fields.name: reason
fields.notes: Brief description of consultation reason; stored as PHI

fields.name: patientId
fields.notes: Only shows patients in the doctor's active patient list

fields.name: instructions
fields.notes: Full usage instructions; stored as PHI

fields.name: patientId
fields.notes: Injected from auth context; patient can only upload for themselves

actions.name: CancelAppointment
actions.confirmationMessage: Are you sure you want to cancel this appointment? Your doctor will be notified.

actions.name: DeactivateUser
actions.confirmationMessage: Deactivating this user will immediately revoke all active sessions. This action is logged. Continue?

authFlows.name: login
authFlows.notes: 2FA/TOTP required for doctor and admin roles. Returns short-lived access token + sets HttpOnly refresh cookie.

authFlows.name: logout
authFlows.notes: Invalidates refresh token server-side and clears HttpOnly cookie.

authFlows.name: refresh
authFlows.notes: Short-lived access token 15min + HttpOnly refresh cookie 7d. Silent refresh handled by Next.js middleware.

authFlows.name: forgot-password
authFlows.notes: Sends time-limited reset link to registered email address.

authFlows.name: reset-password
authFlows.notes: Token expires in 30 minutes (stricter than standard due to HIPAA requirements).

authFlows.name: role-based-access
authFlows.notes: Strict role separation enforced at API level. Patients can only access their own records. Doctors can only access records for patients within their active patient list. Admin has cross-entity read access plus audit log.

uploads.name: MedicalRecordFile
uploads.notes: Files are PHI. Must be stored in private S3 bucket with SSE-S3. Access via pre-signed URL only, short expiry.

externalTriggers.name: VideoSessionWebhook
externalTriggers.expectedOutcome: Update appointment status to completed, record session duration in appointment record.
externalTriggers.notes: Webhook payload must be verified via HMAC-SHA256 signature using DAILY_WEBHOOK_SECRET.

externalTriggers.name: SendAppointmentReminderJob
externalTriggers.expectedOutcome: Send reminder email and SMS to patient with appointment details and video join link.
externalTriggers.notes: Scheduled via Celery Beat. Two separate task triggers per appointment.

externalTriggers.name: SendPrescriptionReadyNotification
externalTriggers.triggerSource: Prescription created by doctor (post-save signal or Celery task)
externalTriggers.expectedOutcome: Notify patient via email that a new prescription is ready to view in the TeleMed portal.
externalTriggers.notes: Email must not include PHI in subject line. Body may include medication name only if BAA with Resend is active.

assumptions.statement: HIPAA compliance required — MedicalRecord, Prescription, and Diagnosis entities contain Protected Health Information (PHI) and must be handled accordingly.
All PHI fields encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM — includes patient name, date of birth, SSN, diagnosis text, and medication name.
Business Associate Agreement (BAA) must be signed with all vendors that process or store PHI: AWS S3, Daily.co, Resend, and Twilio.
Audit log required for all PHI access events, capturing: actor_id, action type, entity_type, entity_id, source IP address, and timestamp.
Doctor-patient relationship enforced at API level — doctors cannot query, view, or modify records for patients outside their own active patient list.
TOTP/2FA is mandatory for all accounts with the doctor or admin role. Login will be blocked at middleware level if TOTP is not configured or not provided.
Medical record files stored in a private AWS S3 bucket with server-side encryption (SSE-S3). No public access. All file downloads served via pre-signed URL.
Appointment video sessions are powered by Daily.co. No video or audio data is stored on TeleMed servers. Session metadata (duration, status) is recorded via webhook.
Password reset tokens expire in 30 minutes. This is stricter than a typical 1-hour window, required by HIPAA minimum necessary and access control standards.

Minimum necessary principle applied to all API responses. Each role receives only the fields required for its function. Doctor endpoints do not expose admin fields; patient endpoints do not expose other patients' data.
